diagrams.title: Frame Allocation Strategies Comparison
diagrams.description: Three approaches to distributing physical memory frames among processes
elements: EQUAL ALLOCATION: All processes get same frames (100/3 = 33 each)
PROPORTIONAL ALLOCATION: Based on process size (large gets more)
PRIORITY ALLOCATION: Critical processes get more frames
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different allocation approaches and when to use each

diagrams.title: Thrashing Cycle
diagrams.description: The circular pattern showing how thrashing occurs and worsens
elements: Low frames → Page fault → Need more frames → Steal from other process → Other process page faults → Both processes thrashing → Low CPU utilization → More I/O wait → More thrashing
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how insufficient frame allocation leads to system degradation

diagrams.title: Working Set Model
diagrams.description: Sliding window approach showing which pages constitute working set
elements: Time axis with sliding window of size Δ
Pages referenced within window = Working Set
Pages outside window = not currently needed
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how OS tracks active pages for frame allocation

diagrams.title: Local vs Global Frame Allocation
diagrams.description: Two different approaches to managing frames for multiple processes
elements: LOCAL: Each process has dedicated frame set (Process A frames, Process B frames)
GLOBAL: Common pool where any process can take any free frame
Shows how replacement policy differs between approaches
